title: Memory Illusion
puzzle: Why can a vivid memory still be wrong?
insight: Memory is not a perfect recording. Each recall rebuilds part of the event, which means confidence and accuracy do not always move together.
key_ideas: Strong emotion can make a memory feel certain without making it precise.
mind_blower: A convincing memory can be partly rewritten every time you revisit it.
next_curiosity: If memory is reconstructed, what does that mean for eyewitness confidence?
narration_text: Why can a vivid memory still be wrong? Memory is not a perfect recording. Each recall rebuilds part of the event, which means confidence and accuracy do not always move together. Memories can shift when retold. Suggestion can influence recollection. Strong emotion can make a memory feel certain without making it precise. A convincing memory can be partly rewritten every time you revisit it. If memory is reconstructed, what does that mean for eyewitness confidence?
